title: carpinus betulus var. carpinizza
intro: Carpinus betulus var. carpinizza, also known as the European hornbeam, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Western Asia. The bark and leaves of the European hornbeam have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to have antioxidant and immune-boosting effects, contributing to its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. European hornbeam can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
